risk_title: Displacement and Refugee Pressures on Education Services
risk_summary: The project is actively serving refugee children through education interventions, with 7,070 refugee children benefiting from direct learning interventions as of September 2024. This indicates ongoing displacement pressures affecting project implementation and service delivery capacity in the region.
evidence_quote: Refugee children benefiting from direct interventions to enhance learning (Number): 7,070 (30-Sep-2024)

risk_title: Flood-Related Displacement and Infrastructure Damage Affecting Implementation
risk_summary: The project includes a dedicated flood relief and rehabilitation component addressing disaster-driven displacement. Flood-affected schools and health facilities require reconstruction and rehabilitation, with significant civil works still in progress, indicating ongoing vulnerability and implementation delays due to conflict/disaster-related infrastructure damage.
evidence_quote: Flood relief and rehabilitation: (Cost 29,000,000.00); Indicator lagging and will not be achieved by June 2025; E&S checklists and ESMPs have been prepared, but works contracts not yet awarded.

risk_title: Political and Governance Instability Affecting Project Implementation
risk_summary: Political and Governance risk is rated as 'High' in the Systematic Operations Risk-Rating Tool, indicating significant governance fragility that is constraining project implementation and achievement of development objectives.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance - Proposed Rating: High; Last Approved Rating: High

risk_title: Institutional Capacity Constraints Linked to Governance Fragility
risk_summary: Fiduciary risk has escalated to 'High' in the current rating, suggesting institutional capacity challenges in implementing agencies (Department of Health and Education) that may be linked to governance fragility, affecting project execution and financial management.
evidence_quote: Fiduciary: Last Approved Rating (ISR Seq. 07 - 14 May 2024): Moderate; Proposed Rating: High
